About Me

Built for This Role

A rare combination of Psychology knowledge, cross-cultural child experience, and proven communication skills.

With a background in Psychology from Universitas Pelita Harapan, I have developed a solid understanding of how children learn, what motivates them at different developmental stages, and how to translate that knowledge into progress conversations that parents find both reassuring and meaningful.

My most formative professional experience was working as a Personal Assistant to a Japanese-Chinese expatriate family in Jakarta — a role that placed me in daily contact with their children during study sessions. I learned to read each child's cues, adapt to their pace, and report observations to parents with the warmth and precision a Progress Advisor needs.

Beyond that role, growing up in a large extended family meant I was always the one the younger children gravitated toward. That instinct — to engage, encourage, and celebrate small wins — has shaped how I approach every child interaction.

On the operational side, I have strong command of Microsoft PowerPoint, Word, and Excel, built through years of producing administrative reports, financial summaries, and project documentation. Creating structured, professional progress presentations is already part of my DNA.

What I Bring to English 1

🧠 Psychology from UPH — Understanding child cognition, behavioral reinforcement, and learning motivation enables me to deliver progress reports that go beyond scores.

💬 Bilingual Communication — Certified B2 English speaker, with daily professional practice in cross-cultural environments.

📊 Documentation & Reporting — Experienced in building structured PowerPoint presentations, Excel records, and CRM-style data management.

❤️ Authentic Care for Children — Not a trait I acquired for this application — it is who I am, shaped by family and two years of daily child interaction.

Child Development Parent Communication PowerPoint Expert B2 English CRM / Data Entry Token Economy
Why English 1 Cipete

The Right Place, The Right Role

Three reasons this opportunity is a strong match — including a very deliberate location choice.

🧒

Passion for Children

Growing up in a large family and spending two-plus years accompanying expatriate children in daily study, I have the patience, adaptability, and genuine enthusiasm to support every child's growth. I do not just track progress — I celebrate every milestone alongside parents.

🤝

Relationship Builder

As Y20 Local Ambassador, environmental campaign lead, and cross-cultural personal assistant, I have built lasting trust with diverse stakeholders. Retaining parents and students at English 1 demands exactly this skill — and it is one I have practiced daily across multiple environments.

📈

Sales & Retention Mindset

My administrative experience at a Pertamina LPG distribution agent trained me to meet targets, manage customer satisfaction, and deliver measurable results. A happy, informed parent is a retained student — and a retained student is a referral waiting to happen.

🚇 Strategic Location: Fatmawati → Cipete

I reside in Fatmawati, South Jakarta — one of the closest residential areas to English 1 Cipete. When exploring this opportunity, I deliberately chose this branch based on its proximity to my home. Consistent on-time attendance, including weekend shifts and peak consultation hours, is something I can fully commit to.

🚇 Via MRT
Fatmawati Station → Cipete Raya Station
2 stations · No traffic dependency
⏱️ ~12 minutes
🚌 Via TransJakarta 1E
Direct service to Cipete
No transfer required · Regular schedule
⏱️ ~18 minutes
Child Experience

Working with Children

Two years of daily interaction with expatriate children — studying, learning, and growing together.

"One of the things that amazes me most about Firda is her determination and adaptability — she never says she cannot do something. Her work ethic, perseverance, and commitment to self-improvement make her an exceptional candidate."

— Maiko Ikeda, Expatriate Client (Japanese-Chinese Family)
📚

Daily Study Accompaniment

Accompanied expatriate children in daily study sessions including English and Japanese workbooks, adapting my approach to each child's pace and learning style.

💬

Parent Communication

Reported children's daily progress to parents consistently — covering learning activities, emotional states, and meaningful small achievements.

🌏

Bilingual Learning Environment

Operated within a Japanese-English-Indonesian household, building cross-cultural communication skills directly relevant to English 1's international learning environment.

🏠

Large Family Background

Growing up surrounded by children of all ages shaped an intuitive understanding of their needs — along with the patience, creativity, and playfulness that young learners respond to.

Sample Work

Sample Progress Reports

Note: The following are sample progress reports illustrating my approach as a Progress Advisor at English 1. All student names and data are fictional. I integrate my Psychology background — specifically the Token Economy system (a positive reinforcement framework grounded in behavioral psychology) — into progress delivery to sustain students' motivation over time.
⭐ Small Stars

Small Stars Program

Ages 3–6 · Pre-reader Level · Focus: Speaking & Phonics

🌟 Naura Azzahra
Age 5 · Small Stars Level 3
SS-3
Speaking Confidence
80%
Phonics Recognition
72%
Vocabulary (Unit 5)
88%
iLAB Completion
65%
🏅 Token Economy — Reward System
⭐⭐⭐⭐☆
Naura has collected 4 out of 5 stars this month. Each star is earned through consistent attendance, completing iLAB homework, and speaking up in class. One more star → a special reward from parents! (Token Economy: a positive behavioral reinforcement system designed to build consistent learning habits in young children.)
🔍 Progress Advisor Note
Naura is most engaged when topics involve animals and colors — she is often the first to answer in class. Parent recommendation: Continue iLAB at least 3× per week at home and use color flashcards for additional reinforcement.
🚀 High Flyers

High Flyers Program

Ages 7–9 · Level HF-2 · Focus: Reading, Writing & Communication

🚀 Rafif Athallah
Age 8 · High Flyers Level 2
HF-2
Listening & Speaking
75%
Reading Comprehension
68%
Writing (Sentence Level)
82%
Grammar Accuracy
70%
🏅 Achievement Tracker
🔵🔵🔵🔵🔵
Rafif earned 5 full blues this month — a perfect streak. He even invited a friend to join iLAB sessions together. This behavior deserves direct recognition from parents and teachers. (Positive reinforcement: external validation strengthens intrinsic motivation in children aged 7–9.)
✅ Renewal Recommendation
Rafif is showing consistent upward growth. Moving to HF Level 3, he will begin simple writing projects and Life Club activities — the ideal preparation for English in secondary school. I am happy to schedule a parent consultation at any convenient time.

* All student names and data above are fictional, created solely to demonstrate my Progress Advisor capabilities. This format and approach will be applied at English 1 using the available CRM system.

🧠 Psychology-Based Competencies

🏅
Token Economy
Applying reward-based reinforcement systems to maintain children's consistent learning motivation across weekly sessions.
🧒
Child Developmental Psychology
Understanding cognitive and emotional development by age group to tailor progress communication to each child's stage.
👂
Empathy & Active Listening
Identifying parent concerns beneath the surface and addressing them with understanding before offering solutions.
⚡
Motivation & Behavioral Reinforcement
Using positive reinforcement strategies to sustain both student engagement and parent confidence in the learning journey.
